title: Well-known LLM gotchas (exact answers)
system_prompt: You are an expert at reasoning and you always give the most accurate answer. Think step by step and take as much reasoning as you need; do not rush. You MUST finish with a line in exactly this format: Final Answer: X where X is your answer (a word, number, or short phrase). Do not stop before writing that final line.
license: questions are common-knowledge facts; see LICENSES.md

questions.prompt: How many times does the letter R appear in the word STRAWBERRY?
How many letters are in the word STRAWBERRY?
Which is larger: 9.11 or 9.9?
Which is larger: 8.11 or 8.9?
Sort these numbers from smallest to largest: 9.9, 9.11, 9.1
A man has no brothers or sisters. He looks at a photograph and says: "That person's father is my father's son." Who is in the photograph?
If a rooster lays an egg on top of a pointed roof, which side does the egg roll down?
A father and his son are in a car accident. The father dies at the scene. The son is rushed to the hospital and the doctor says: "I cannot operate on this boy, he is my son." How is this possible?
How many months have 28 days?
How many letters are in the phrase "the alphabet"?
What is half of twelve?
I have two coins that add up to 30 cents. One of them is not a nickel. What are the two coins?
You have 30 sick sheep and one sheep dies. How many sheep do you have left?
What is the only word that is spelled incorrectly in every dictionary?
Tom's father is three times as old as Tom. In 12 years Tom's father will be twice as old as Tom. How old is Tom now?
How many letters are in the word BANANA?
How many times does the letter S appear in MISSISSIPPI?
How many times does the letter I appear in MISSISSIPPI?

Two volumes of an encyclopedia stand upright and closed on a shelf, side by side in normal reading order (Volume 1 on the left, Volume 2 on its right), each shelved the usual way so the pages face the same direction as they would on a bookstore display. Every volume is 2 inches of pages bound between two 0.25-inch-thick covers. A bookworm bores in a straight horizontal line from the front cover of Volume 1 through to the back cover of Volume 2, taking the shortest path. In inches, what is the total thickness of material the worm eats through?
A woman is born in 1980, is currently 43 years old, and her birthday is not today. What year does she turn 44?
How many letters are in the word PENCIL?
Which number is larger: 0.999 or 1?
When does tomorrow come before yesterday?
What is 99 multiplied by 0?
